description: Evaluate the top proxy-calibrated TokenMonster candidate english-1024-balanced-v1, which showed the lowest mean postquant_val_bpb (4.102) and lowest variance (std 0.145) among all tested vocabularies.

decision.headline: TokenMonster balanced-v1 beats baseline by 0.23 calibrated BPB
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.139 vs baseline 4.368 is a substantial 5.2% improvement, well outside the noise range (std ~0.15-0.19). TokenMonster balanced-v1 also has dramatically fewer dead vocab entries (4.6% vs 14.2%), meaning better utilization of the embedding parameter budget. The tokens_per_byte is slightly better too, giving marginally shorter sequences for the same text.
decision.frontier_update: New frontier is english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. TokenMonster 1024-vocab family clearly dominates sentencepiece 1024. The clean-v1 and consistent-v1 variants are close behind and worth evaluating for confirmation, but balanced-v1 is the candidate to beat.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 to confirm balanced-v1 is the best TokenMonster 1024 variant before integrating into train_gpt.py

description: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 to compare against balanced-v1 winner

decision.headline: clean-v1 scores 4.209 vs balanced-v1's 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: english-1024-clean-v1 has a calibrated score of 4.209, which is 0.070 worse than the frontier (balanced-v1 at 4.139). While clean-v1 has slightly better compression (tokens_per_byte 0.405 vs 0.408) and marginally fewer dead vocab entries, the proxy calibration data shows clean-v1 has higher variance (std 0.234 vs 0.145 for balanced-v1), which compounds into a worse risk-adjusted score. The result confirms balanced-v1 is the superior 1024-vocab TokenMonster variant.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. Two of three TokenMonster 1024 variants now evaluated; balanced-v1 leads clean-v1 by a clear margin. consistent-v1 is the last variant to confirm.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-consistent-v1 to complete the 1024-vocab comparison, then move to integrating balanced-v1 into train_gpt.py

description: Evaluate english-1024-consistent-v1 to complete the 1024 TokenMonster variant comparison

decision.headline: consistent-v1 (4.195) does not beat balanced-v1 frontier (4.139)
decision.reasoning: The consistent-v1 variant scores 4.195 calibrated BPB, which is 0.057 worse than the balanced-v1 frontier at 4.139. While consistent-v1 has slightly better compression (tokens_per_byte 0.4074 vs 0.4082) and fewer dead vocab entries (3.5% vs 4.6%), these marginal tokenizer-level advantages do not overcome the calibrated BPB gap. All three TokenMonster 1024 variants have now been evaluated, confirming balanced-v1 as the best.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. All three TokenMonster 1024 variants are now evaluated: balanced-v1 (4.139) > consistent-v1 (4.195) > clean-v1 (needs eval or use proxy). The 1024 TokenMonster family search is complete.
decision.next_direction: Integrate english-1024-balanced-v1 into train_gpt.py and run an end-to-end training validation to confirm the proxy-calibrated improvement translates to real val_bpb gains.

description: SentencePiece BPE at vocab 768 — test whether a smaller vocabulary reduces proxy BPB by cutting dead vocab and parameter overhead, since calibration strongly favors smaller tokenizers

decision.headline: SP-768 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — worse by 0.23 calibrated BPB
decision.reasoning: The smaller SentencePiece vocab at 768 scores 4.368, well behind the TokenMonster balanced-v1 frontier at 4.139. While it achieves near-zero dead vocab and saves ~36KB in artifact budget, the higher tokens_per_byte (0.442 vs 0.408) destroys any parameter savings advantage. The calibration already accounts for artifact budget differences, and the net score is decisively worse.
decision.frontier_update: Confirms that SentencePiece family is uncompetitive even at reduced vocab sizes. The TokenMonster 1024 family remains dominant. Shrinking vocab below 1024 hurts sequence efficiency more than it helps via parameter savings.
decision.next_direction: Head-to-head the remaining TokenMonster 1024 variants (clean-v1 vs consistent-v1) against balanced-v1 to lock in the best candidate before integration.

description: SentencePiece BPE 1024 with larger training sample (1M sentences) to improve subword quality without changing vocab size — testing if better training data coverage can close the gap with TokenMonster balanced-v1

decision.headline: Retrained SentencePiece still loses to TokenMonster balanced-v1 by 0.23 calibrated BPB
decision.reasoning: The retrained SentencePiece 1024 scores 4.368 calibrated vs the frontier at 4.139 — a 0.23 gap. While it eliminated dead vocab (0.4% vs 14% baseline), it actually increased tokens_per_byte (+0.006 vs baseline), producing a worse budget_aware_score (0.427 vs baseline 0.423). Better SentencePiece training data cannot close the fundamental gap with TokenMonster balanced-v1.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at english-1024-balanced-v1 (4.139). This confirms SentencePiece 1024 is structurally inferior to TokenMonster 1024 — even with optimal training, the family-level calibration gap (~0.17 BPB) holds. Stop exploring SentencePiece 1024 variants.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 to confirm balanced-v1 is the best TokenMonster 1024 variant before integration

description: SentencePiece unigram at 1024 vocab — different model type may produce better subword segmentation than BPE, potentially closing the gap with TokenMonster balanced-v1

decision.headline: Unigram SentencePiece worse than both baseline BPE and TokenMonster balanced-v1
decision.reasoning: Calibrated score 4.368 is worse than the frontier 4.139 (balanced-v1) and even worse than the baseline SentencePiece BPE at 4.320. The unigram model produces more tokens per byte (0.430 vs 0.411 baseline), meaning worse compression. While dead vocab is near zero (0.3%), this doesn't compensate for the substantially worse segmentation quality.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. SentencePiece unigram is a dead end — both BPE and unigram SentencePiece variants lose convincingly to TokenMonster at 1024 vocab. The SentencePiece family appears exhausted at this vocab size.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 vs balanced-v1 head-to-head to confirm the best TokenMonster variant before integrating into train_gpt.py

description: SentencePiece BPE 896 vocab to explore between 768 (too few) and 1024 baseline

decision.headline: SP-896 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The SentencePiece 896-vocab tokenizer scores 4.368 calibrated, which is 0.23 worse than the TokenMonster balanced-v1 frontier at 4.139. While it saves ~18KB in artifact budget and has near-zero dead vocab, the tokens_per_byte regression (0.430 vs 0.408) is too large. The SP family at 1024 already scored 4.320, so shrinking to 896 made it worse as expected.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at english-1024-balanced-v1 (4.139). SentencePiece BPE at 896 confirms that sub-1024 SP vocabs move in the wrong direction. The TokenMonster 1024 family remains clearly dominant over all SentencePiece variants tested.
decision.next_direction: Try TokenMonster 768 or 896 balanced/clean variants to see if smaller TM vocabs can trade artifact savings for competitive scores.

description: SentencePiece BPE 1024 with larger training corpus (500k sentences) to improve merge quality while staying at baseline vocab size

decision.headline: Larger SP corpus still loses to TokenMonster balanced-v1 by 0.23 BPB
decision.reasoning: The new SentencePiece 1024 trained on 500k sentences scores 4.368 calibrated BPB, well above the frontier of 4.139 (english-1024-balanced-v1). While it nearly eliminates dead vocab (0.4% vs 14.2% baseline), it actually produces more tokens per byte than the baseline SP tokenizer, indicating worse merge quality despite the larger corpus. The sentencepiece:1024 family proxy mean (4.320) already suggested this ceiling.
decision.frontier_update: No change. TokenMonster balanced-v1 remains the frontier at 4.139. This confirms that scaling SP training data alone cannot close the ~0.18 BPB gap to TokenMonster. The sentencepiece:1024 family appears exhausted as a search direction.
decision.next_direction: Compare english-1024-clean-v1 vs english-1024-balanced-v1 head-to-head to confirm the best TokenMonster variant before integration into train_gpt.py.

description: Test very small SentencePiece 512 BPE — fewer vocab-linked params may offset worse tokenization via budget-aware scoring, and no proxy calibration exists for this size so text metrics will matter more

decision.headline: SP-512 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The 512-vocab SentencePiece tokenizer scores 4.368 calibrated BPB, which is 0.23 worse than the frontier (english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139). The artifact savings from halving vocab-linked params (~73KB) are far too small to offset the 19% worse tokens-per-byte (0.488 vs 0.408). Even without proxy calibration for this vocab size, the raw text metrics clearly show this is a losing trade.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. Shrinking vocab below 1024 is a dead end — the tokenization quality penalty dominates any artifact budget savings at this scale.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 to confirm balanced-v1 is the best TokenMonster 1024 variant.

description: SentencePiece BPE at vocab 1152 — nearest unexplored size above baseline, testing whether a modest vocab bump improves tokens_per_byte enough to shift the calibrated score within the SP family

decision.headline: SP-1152 scores 4.368, well behind balanced-v1 frontier at 4.139
decision.reasoning: The SentencePiece 1152-vocab tokenizer scores 4.368 calibrated BPB, which is 0.229 worse than the current frontier (english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139). While it improves over the SP-1024 baseline (4.320), it still falls far short of the TokenMonster 1024 family. The modest tokens_per_byte improvement from the vocab bump does not overcome the SentencePiece family's calibrated disadvantage.
decision.frontier_update: No change. TokenMonster 1024 balanced-v1 remains the clear frontier. This confirms that scaling SP vocab size modestly does not close the gap with TokenMonster — the family-level calibration penalty for sentencepiece is too large.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 head-to-head to confirm balanced-v1 is the best TokenMonster 1024 variant.

description: SentencePiece BPE at vocab 1280 — slightly above baseline scale, testing whether moderate vocab growth with BPE can close the gap to TokenMonster balanced-v1

decision.headline: SP-1280 scores 4.368, well behind TokenMonster balanced-v1 at 4.139
decision.reasoning: The SentencePiece 1280-vocab experiment scored 4.368 calibrated BPB, which is 0.23 worse than the frontier (TokenMonster balanced-v1 at 4.139) and only marginally better than the baseline SP-1024 at 4.320. Growing SentencePiece vocab to 1280 improves tokens_per_byte but adds artifact cost and still cannot close the gap to TokenMonster. This confirms the SentencePiece family is structurally disadvantaged at this vocab scale.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at TokenMonster balanced-v1 (4.139). SentencePiece BPE at 1280 vocab provides further evidence that scaling SP vocab modestly does not overcome the TokenMonster advantage. The SP family appears exhausted as a search direction at 1024-1280 vocab sizes.
decision.next_direction: Abandon SentencePiece variants and focus on confirming english-1024-clean-v1 vs balanced-v1, or explore TokenMonster at slightly different configurations.

description: SentencePiece BPE 1024 with lower character_coverage (0.999) to produce a tighter, more focused vocabulary that may reduce dead tokens and improve proxy-calibrated score

decision.headline: SentencePiece 0.999 coverage scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is 0.229 worse than the frontier (english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139). While dead vocab dropped dramatically to 0.4%, the tokens_per_byte actually increased slightly, and the sentencepiece:1024 family calibration penalty (~4.32 mean) makes this uncompetitive against TokenMonster 1024 variants (~4.15 mean). Tuning SentencePiece hyperparameters cannot overcome the family-level gap.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at english-1024-balanced-v1 (4.139). This confirms that SentencePiece BPE 1024 tuning is a dead end — even near-zero dead vocab cannot close the ~0.17 family gap against TokenMonster 1024.
decision.next_direction: Abandon SentencePiece 1024 tuning and evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 vs balanced-v1 head-to-head to confirm the best TokenMonster variant before integration.

description: Evaluate TokenMonster 2048-clean-v1 to check if the larger vocab hurts calibrated score as expected from proxy data (mean 4.50 vs 4.10-4.15 for 1024 variants), confirming we should stay at 1024 scale

decision.headline: 2048-vocab confirmed worse than 1024 variants, as expected from proxy data
decision.reasoning: The 2048-clean-v1 scored 4.563 calibrated BPB, far worse than the frontier 4.139 (balanced-v1 1024). This matches the proxy prediction (mean 4.50 for 2048 vs 4.10-4.15 for 1024). The larger vocab doubles embedding parameters (+524K params, +146KB artifact) for worse compression efficiency. No reason to keep.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. The 2048-vocab avenue is now empirically closed — larger TokenMonster vocabs hurt both calibrated score and artifact budget. Search should stay at 1024 scale.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 head-to-head to confirm balanced-v1 is the best 1024 variant before integration into train_gpt.py.

description: SentencePiece BPE at vocab 1536 — tests whether a moderately larger SP vocab can overcome the calibration penalty, since all 1024-class SP experiments map to the same proxy bucket

decision.headline: SP-1536 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The SentencePiece 1536-vocab experiment scores 4.368 calibrated BPB, which is 0.23 worse than the frontier (TokenMonster balanced-v1 at 4.139). The larger vocab also costs 262K extra params and ~73KB more artifact space. Even though tokens_per_byte improved, the proxy calibration penalty for sentencepiece and the extra artifact cost make this clearly inferior.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at TokenMonster english-1024-balanced-v1 (4.139). This result confirms that scaling SP vocab to 1536 does not overcome the family-level calibration gap versus TokenMonster 1024. The sentencepiece family appears exhausted at these vocab sizes.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 to confirm balanced-v1 is the best TokenMonster 1024 variant.

description: SentencePiece BPE at vocab 960, slightly below baseline scale — tests whether a modestly smaller vocabulary with BPE can close the gap to TokenMonster 1024 by saving vocab budget while staying near-baseline token efficiency

decision.headline: SP BPE 960 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is 0.23 worse than the frontier (english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139). The modest artifact savings from 1024→960 vocab (~9KB) do not compensate for the worse token efficiency and higher tokens_per_byte. SentencePiece BPE at this scale consistently underperforms TokenMonster variants.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. This result reinforces that sentencepiece BPE cannot close the gap to TokenMonster even with slightly reduced vocab. The sentencepiece family appears exhausted at 1024-scale.
decision.next_direction: Focus on TokenMonster 1024 variants — evaluate clean-v1 and consistent-v1 head-to-head to confirm balanced-v1 is optimal before integration.

description: SentencePiece unigram at 768 vocab — smaller vocab with unigram model type, untried combination that may produce a different token distribution than BPE at this scale

decision.headline: SP unigram 768 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is 0.23 worse than the frontier (4.139 from TokenMonster balanced-v1). The smaller vocab saves ~36KB artifact space but the tokens_per_byte penalty (+0.046) far outweighs this. SentencePiece at any vocab size has consistently underperformed TokenMonster 1024 variants in calibration.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. This result further confirms that SentencePiece (both BPE and unigram) and smaller vocabs are not competitive against TokenMonster 1024. The search should stop exploring SentencePiece variants.
decision.next_direction: Focus on confirming balanced-v1 vs clean-v1 vs consistent-v1 TokenMonster variants, then integrate the winner into train_gpt.py.

description: SentencePiece unigram at 960 vocab — testing if unigram model type at a slightly sub-baseline scale can close the gap with TokenMonster balanced-v1

decision.headline: SentencePiece unigram 960 scores 4.368, well behind frontier 4.139
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is 0.23 worse than the frontier (english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139). Despite near-zero dead vocab and slightly smaller artifact size, the tokens_per_byte regression (+0.024 vs baseline) confirms unigram at this scale cannot compete with TokenMonster balanced-v1. The small vocab savings (~9KB) is irrelevant given the large BPB gap.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at english-1024-balanced-v1 (4.139). SentencePiece unigram is now ruled out alongside BPE — the SP family appears structurally inferior to TokenMonster at 1024-scale for this task.
decision.next_direction: Try training a custom SentencePiece BPE at 1024 with aggressive character coverage tuning, or pivot fully to confirming TokenMonster balanced-v1 integration into train_gpt.py.

description: SentencePiece unigram at 1152 vocab — testing if unigram model type at a slightly above-baseline scale can improve tokens_per_byte enough to matter, while staying near the 1024 baseline

decision.headline: SentencePiece unigram 1152 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — no improvement
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is substantially worse than the current frontier of 4.139 (balanced-v1). The unigram model type produces worse tokens_per_byte (0.420 vs 0.408) despite the larger vocab, and the extra 128 vocab entries add artifact budget without compensating. SentencePiece as a family consistently underperforms TokenMonster 1024 in the proxy calibration data (4.320 vs 4.151 family means).
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. This further confirms that SentencePiece variants — even with alternative model types and slightly larger vocabs — cannot close the gap to TokenMonster 1024.
decision.next_direction: Focus remaining experiments on TokenMonster 1024 variants or novel compression approaches rather than further SentencePiece exploration.

description: SentencePiece unigram at 1280 vocab — testing whether unigram model type at slightly above baseline scale can close the gap with TokenMonster balanced-v1. Unigram has different merge behavior that may produce better subword units at this scale.

decision.headline: SP unigram 1280 scores 4.368 vs frontier 4.139 — not competitive
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is substantially worse than the frontier (4.139), a gap of ~0.23. The larger vocab (1280 vs 1024) also costs more artifact budget without any compensating quality gain. SentencePiece unigram does not close the gap with TokenMonster balanced-v1.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier remains english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139. SentencePiece unigram at 1280 vocab is now ruled out. The sentencepiece family continues to underperform TokenMonster at the 1024 scale, and increasing vocab size to 1280 with unigram model type does not help.
decision.next_direction: Try TokenMonster 1024 variants (clean-v1, consistent-v1) head-to-head to confirm balanced-v1 is optimal, or explore sub-1024 TokenMonster vocabs to test if a smaller tokenizer can match performance.

description: SentencePiece BPE 1024 with 2M input sentences to test if substantially more training data improves tokenizer quality and calibrated proxy score

decision.headline: SentencePiece 2M-sentence BPE 1024 scores 4.368, well behind frontier 4.139
decision.reasoning: The calibrated score of 4.368 is 0.23 points worse than the frontier (english-1024-balanced-v1 at 4.139). While this SentencePiece variant has near-zero dead vocab (0.4% vs 14.2% baseline), its higher tokens_per_byte and the sentencepiece:1024 family's weaker calibration coefficient make it uncompetitive. More training sentences did not close the gap with TokenMonster.
decision.frontier_update: Frontier unchanged at english-1024-balanced-v1 (4.139). This confirms that even well-trained SentencePiece 1024 cannot match TokenMonster 1024 variants. The SentencePiece axis is exhausted for vocab_size=1024.
decision.next_direction: Evaluate english-1024-clean-v1 and english-1024-consistent-v1 to confirm balanced-v1 is the best TokenMonster 1024 variant before integration.
